Reviews: Works well in everything EXCEPT one major bug it's always had which sometimes gets better, sometimes (currently) gets much worse: the audio only comes in part way into a paragraph. And when having it read emails out loud where someone breaks every sentence into paragraphs, you can miss half of every sentence! This thing never reads the beginning of a paragraph out loud - ever.
This was the best for me for a long time. But now it refuses to work at all. I have dyslexia and I HAVE to have something. So now I have to use something else. It was nice while it lasted!

I tried several readers, and this is by far the best.  Hate the "natural voices" on all readers, which is good for this one as their default is among the few that work at 2x speed.  Until last week or so it was worth a shining 5 star review, which I had been procrastinating on.  Inexplicably, it recently started reading "dot" and "comma" which I very much do not care for and cannot find a way to remove from playback.  Would love that to go back to being short pauses, but the settings menu is lacking any such toggles.

Almost perfect.  What would make it better is a natural pause between paragraphs, after bullet points, and cells in tables.  Without that natural pause (similar to when there is punctuation), the reader runs text together that don't belong together into one giant sentence.  For example, the heading of a section with the first sentence of the section, all the bullet points in a list, or all the cells in a table become one giant sentence.  To fix this, treat a new paragraph, the end of a bullet point, or a new cell in a table like punctuation, and pause. A nice to have would be a text highlighter following along with the reader. Had to come back and take away one star in March 2026 because the reader stopped reading hyperlinked texted.  It just skips that part of a sentence.

Great app, but when in doubt it reads in Japanese instead of Chinese. I hope there would be a way to make the chinese the default way. For example, when asked to read this: 先生 it would read it in Japanese but when I show it this all together: 先生，您好 it would read it in Chinese.
Unfourtunately I could not find lovely Persian language in that so I remove it. I hope you add it for soon

I like this extension. I've used it for years. I really enjoyed their use of localized voices that didn't rely on AI. Nowadays, however, any time I want to use the extension, I have to give it extra permissions-- this didn't used to be the case. Once I give it permission (to use google voices), the extension works for the rest of my session, in-browser. Unfortunately, every time I close out of the browser, I have to do it all over again.
it always works perfectly.. but since some days ago just stop working at all

Default robot voices works for swedish. Absolutely nothing else works. It just hangs. Edge already supports the robot voice, so this app literally serves no purpose except to waste your time with the hope of getting additional voice support only to instead waste your time.

Good extension. It does what it is supposed to do but, IMHO, has a huge improvement opportunity. When a line ends without a period, for example, on a title or in bullet points, the reader continues reading the next line as if it were part of the previous one.

love this so much! voice speed, volume, voice, and pitch are adjustable. now, this only works for main texts and hasn't shown to read side texts like the little "table of contents" box that sits on the top of your screen. but since that is not what I need it for, it works better than any other site I've used as it doesn't require a subscription to use its full features!
